title: bitwarden OnceCell caches auth — permanent failure after token expiry
description: Scenario: a BitwardenBackend is constructed with access_token A. The first get() authenticates and populates the OnceCell with (client, org_id). Later, token A expires or is revoked. All subsequent get() calls use the cached (client, org_id) and hit the Bitwarden API, which returns auth errors. The OnceCell is permanently set; there is no way to trigger re-auth without constructing a new BitwardenBackend. Maintenance consequence: user files issue 'Bitwarden backend stops working after N hours'; support burden. The previous re-auth-per-call behavior was self-healing. The new behavior is not. The fix is NOT to remove the cache (that's the right optimization) but to document the limitation clearly: 'if the access token is rotated, construct a new BitwardenBackend; the auth session is cached for the lifetime of the object.' Also check whether the bitwarden_sdk Client has a refresh or re-auth method.
